title: Fourth Quarter 2025 Survey of Professional Forecasters
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The Fourth Quarter 2025 Survey of Professional Forecasters showed slight upward revisions for 2025 inflation but stable expectations for 2026-2027, with GDP gro
excerpt: The Fourth Quarter 2025 Survey of Professional Forecasters showed slight upward revisions for 2025 inflation but stable expectations for 2026-2027, with GDP growth at 1.2% for 2025. The survey also indicated balanced risks and unchanged uncertainty for longer-term inflation expectations.

content.introduction: The Fourth Quarter 2025 Survey of Professional Forecasters showed slight upward revisions for 2025 inflation but stable expectations for 2026-2027, with GDP growth at 1.2% for 2025. The survey also indicated balanced risks and unchanged uncertainty for longer-term inflation expectations.
body: In the ECB’s Survey of Professional Forecasters (SPF) for the fourth quarter of 2025,[1] expectations for headline HICP inflation were revised slightly upwards for 2025 but remained unchanged for 2026, 2027 and the longer term. **Regarding baseline inflation expectations, tariffs were expected to have a minor downward impact in the nearer term (-0.04 and -0.06 percentage points for 2025 and 2026 respectively), but to be broadly neutral on balance in 2027 and over the longer-term (2030) horizon (Chart A, panel a, blue line).** Compared with the previous SPF round, this represents relatively small revisions across all horizons. **Uncertainty surrounding longer-term inflation expectations was largely unchanged and the balance of risks remained close to being broadly balanced.** Aggregate uncertainty (the standard deviation of the aggregate probability distribution) was largely unchanged in this round (Chart 5). **GDP growth expectations stood at 1.2% for 2025, 1.1% for 2026 and 1.4% for 2027 (Chart 6).** Compared with the previous round, these figures imply a slight upward revision of 0.1 percentage points for 2025, while the outlook for 2026 and 2027 remained broadly unchanged.

*   [Survey of Professional Forecasters](https://www.philadelphiafed.org/surveys-and-data/real-time-data-research/survey-of-professional-forecasters).
